channel.title: economics on errbufferoverfl
channel.description: Recent content in economics on errbufferoverfl
channel.copyright: This content is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International unless otherwise specified.

item.title: Ghost Work
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: “A startling exposé of the invisible human workforce that powers the web–and how to bring it out of the shadows. Hidden beneath the surface of the internet, a new, stark reality is looming–one that cuts to the very heart of our endless debates about the impact of AI.

item.title: structural adjustment programs
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: Structural adjustment programs are a [[notes/neocolonialism|neocolonist]] tool that sets out a set of economic reforms that a country must adhere to secure a loan from the International Monetary Fund and/or the World Bank.

item.title: The Age of Surveillance Capitalism
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: The challenges to humanity posed by the digital future, the first detailed examination of the unprecedented form of power called ‘surveillance capitalism,’ and the quest by powerful corporations to predict and control us.

item.title: reproductive labor
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: Reproductive labor was first brought into the discourse during the wages for housework campaign during the 1970s (discussed in Revolution at Point Zero).

item.title: Lost in Work Escaping Capitalism
item.author: errbuffoverfl (errbuffoverfl)
item.description: ‘Work hard, get paid.’ It’s simple. Self-evident. But it’s also a lie—at least for most of us. For young people today, the old assumptions are crumbling; hard work in school no longer guarantees a secure, well-paying job in the future.
